passages.passage_text: Direction (Q. No. 22) Read the following information carefully to answer the question that follows. 'P -Q' means 'Q is father of P'. 'P * Q' means 'Q is brother of P'. 'P + Q' means 'Q is wife of P'. 'P × Q' means 'Q is sister of P'.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: If it is given 'P × R – Q', then which of the following is true?

choices.a: P is the brother-in-law of Q
choices.b: P is the brother of Q
choices.c: P is the uncle of Q
choices.d: P is the father of Q

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: If 'P + R × Q', then which of the following is true?

choices.a: P is the husband of Q
choices.b: P is the brother of Q
choices.c: P is the son of Q
choices.d: P is the father of Q

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: If 'P – R × Q', then which of the following is true?

choices.a: P is the sister of Q
choices.b: Q is the son of P
choices.c: Q is the husband of P
choices.d: P is the sister-in-law of Q

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: If 'P – Q', means Q is son of P, 'P × Q' means P is brother of Q, 'P + Q' means Q is sister of P and 'P + Q' means P is mother of Q, then which of the following is definitely true about 'N × K – M + L'? [SNAP 2013]

choices.a: K is father of L and M
choices.b: L is daughter of K and niece of uncle N
choices.c: K is father of L and M, his son and daughter respectively
choices.d: M is uncle of K's brother N

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Which of the following means M is the grandfather of T?
